response: A powerful magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck near Tokyo, Japan (35.6°N, 139.7°E) on March 15, 2024, at 14:23 UTC, with a relatively shallow depth of 15 kilometers. The disaster has affected approximately 2.3 million people, resulted in 12 confirmed casualties, and caused an estimated $4.5 billion in damage. Authorities have issued a tsunami warning in response to the seismic event.
